fieldRegistry.label: Primary Reason For Home Health / Medical Necessity

fieldGroups.label: Medical Necessity And Homebound
fieldGroups.description: Narratives and evidence for home health need and homebound status.

fieldGroups.label: Diagnosis And Coding
fieldGroups.description: Diagnosis evidence, coding-sensitive rows, and escalation flags.

fieldGroups.label: Medications, Allergies, And Special Treatments
fieldGroups.description: Medication safety, allergies, injectables, and O0110 special treatments.

fieldGroups.label: Functional And Therapy Status
fieldGroups.description: GG/M functional scoring, therapy need, discipline frequency, and goals.

fieldGroups.label: Symptom And Body System Review
fieldGroups.description: Body-system review sections that QA checks for consistency and completeness.

fieldGroups.label: Risk And Safety Assessments
fieldGroups.description: Fall, wound, hospitalization, and safety-related risk tools.

fieldGroups.label: Care Plan And Coordination
fieldGroups.description: Plan-of-care orders, patient summary, skilled interventions, and care plan content.

fieldGroups.label: Supplementals And History
fieldGroups.description: Past medical history, caregiver support, living situation, immunizations, and supplementals.

fieldGroups.label: Low Priority Admin Reference
fieldGroups.description: Identity, agency, physician, and demographic reference values that should not dominate QA.

sourceHighlights.title: Homebound reason
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient requires minimal assistance for bed mobility, transfers, and ambulation with a front-wheeled walker, and needs assistance with ADLs, indicating homebound status.

sourceHighlights.title: Medical necessity
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient requires intensive rehabilitation therapy in an inpatient hospital environment due to the complexity of nursing, medical management, and rehabilitation needs.

sourceHighlights.title: Prior level of function
sourceHighlights.summary: Prior to the current illness, the patient was independent in mobility, self-care, and cognition, and maintained a moderately active lifestyle.

sourceHighlights.title: Wound history
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient has stage 1 pressure ulcers on the sacrum and right hip, with erythema but no drainage.

sourceHighlights.title: Diet and fluid instructions
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient is on a heart-healthy, sodium-restricted diet with daily Ensure supplement and juice.

sourceHighlights.title: PMH, immunizations, and DM status
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient has a past medical history of CHF, COPD, CAD, HTN, HLD, hyperthyroidism, and obesity class I.

sourceHighlights.title: Diagnoses and possible coding support references
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient has diagnoses of COPD exacerbation, acute on chronic respiratory failure with pneumonia, CHF exacerbation, NSTEMI, impaired gait, mobility, and endurance, and requires assistance with ADLs.

draftNarratives.label: Homebound Reason
draftNarratives.draft: The patient requires minimal assistance for bed mobility, transfers, and ambulation with a front-wheeled walker, and needs assistance with ADLs, indicating homebound status.

draftNarratives.label: Medical Necessity
draftNarratives.draft: The patient requires intensive rehabilitation therapy in an inpatient hospital environment due to the complexity of nursing, medical management, and rehabilitation needs.

draftNarratives.label: Patient Summary / Clinical Narrative
draftNarratives.draft: Diane Pastirik is a 65-year-old female with a past medical history of CHF, COPD, CAD, HTN, HLD, and hyperthyroidism. She was admitted to the hospital with acute on chronic respiratory failure, pneumonia, CHF exacerbation, and NSTEMI. Following her hospitalization, she experienced a significant decline in functional status, now requiring minimal assistance for bed mobility, transfers, ambulation with a front-wheeled walker, and basic ADLs. She is now admitted to Encompass acute inpatient rehab for a comprehensive therapy program prior to safe discharge home.

draftNarratives.label: Skilled Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Occupational Therapy Discharge Recommendations Physical Therapy Discharge Recommendations Occupational Therapy.

draftNarratives.draft: Occupational Therapy Discharge Recommendations Physical Therapy Discharge Recommendations Occupational Therapy.

draftNarratives.label: Care Plan Problems / Goals / Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: The patient requires intensive rehabilitation therapy in an inpatient hospital environment due to the complexity of nursing, medical management, and rehabilitation needs.

draftNarratives.label: Patient / Caregiver Goals
